articles.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
articles.summary: Folk musician Murphy Campbell discovered unauthorized AI-generated covers of her songs on Spotify, created from her YouTube performances. This case highlights vulnerabilities in copyright systems and AI misuse targeting independent artists.

articles.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
articles.summary: As AI becomes increasingly sophisticated at mimicking human work, platforms struggle with content labeling. The article suggests implementing universal labels to certify human-made content, similar to Fair Trade certification.

articles.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
articles.summary: Anthropic will require Claude users to pay separately for OpenClaw access starting April 4th, ending integration with their subscription. Users must switch to a pay-as-you-go billing model.

articles.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
articles.summary: Anthropic will charge Claude Code subscribers additional fees to use OpenClaw and third-party tools with their coding assistant.

articles.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
articles.summary: Anthropic dominates private share trading while OpenAI loses momentum. SpaceX's upcoming IPO could significantly reshape the private market landscape.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
articles.summary: OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap takes on new special projects role. CMO Kate Rouch departs temporarily for cancer recovery, planning to return later.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS with a platform that has attracted 2 million developers. The investment capitalizes on developer demand for simpler alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers free open-source alternative with similar functionality running locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, has security vulnerabilities. The tool requires broad system access to perform tasks, creating significant security risks for users.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Researchers discovered three new Rowhammer attacks exploiting memory bit flips in Nvidia GPUs, allowing malicious cloud users to gain complete root control of shared machines.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Quantum computers require significantly fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic curve encryption. New research shows quantum systems could crack 256-bit cryptography in 10 days with 100x less overhead.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX filed to launch one million orbital data centers to support AI without environmental impact on Earth. Tech giants like Amazon and Google are also exploring space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from global conflict threaten plastic supply chains, as petrochemicals become scarcer and more expensive, likely impacting American consumers.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from Middle East tensions threaten plastic supply chains, as petrochemicals become scarcer. Plastic production's widespread use in daily products could face significant economic and environmental impacts.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on devices. The model advances on-device AI intelligence capabilities.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use AI, advancing the frontier of how artificial intelligence interacts with digital systems and interfaces.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: No article content provided to summarize.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential impact on various industries and society.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Major brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities. These AI solutions enable companies to streamline content creation and imaginative processes.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for 'farm of the future' to advance agricultural technology and sustainability through AI-powered farming solutions.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google launched two new inference tiers for the Gemini API: Flex and Priority, designed to balance cost and latency for users.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technology, enabling users to generate high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to help protect and monitor the country's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, delivering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves audio AI with enhanced precision and reduced latency for more natural and fluid conversations.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind investigates how AI can be manipulated to cause harm in finance and healthcare sectors, implementing new safety measures to prevent misuse.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and claimed token-efficiency improvements. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI development.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting. The technology marks a significant advancement in blending traditional rendering with AI for photorealism.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR review pricing, and Cursor's coding automations. New interactive visualization features were announced by ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw addresses shadow AI by providing enterprises governance over autonomous agents that employees deploy independently. The platform tackles BYOAI practices that expose proprietary data to unregulated external environments.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Key practices include strict access governance, role-based permissions, and encryption to protect sensitive AI models.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizes AI development through 2030, focusing on high-performance chips, software, and advanced model architectures. The plan positions AI alongside quantum computing and biotechnology as strategic technologies supported by communications infrastructure.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing key debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI model performance.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering and parabolic extrapolation enable LLMs to function as theoretical collaborators, producing testable multiverse models for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Netflix AI Team Just Open-Sourced VOID: an AI Model That Erases Objects From Videos — Physics and All
articles.summary: Netflix's VOID AI model automatically removes objects from videos while realistically handling physics interactions, like objects falling when a person is deleted. This solves the longstanding video editing challenge of making removals look natural.

articles.title: How to Build Production-Ready Agentic Systems with Z.AI GLM-5 Using Thinking Mode, Tool Calling, Streaming, and Multi-Turn Workflows
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building production-ready agentic systems using Z.AI's GLM-5 model, covering features like thinking mode, tool calling, streaming, and multi-turn workflows for scalable AI applications.

articles.title: Google DeepMind’s Research Lets an LLM Rewrite Its Own Game Theory Algorithms — And It Outperformed the Experts
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve uses LLMs to automatically design game theory algorithms for multi-agent reinforcement learning, outperforming manually-created versions in imperfect-information games like poker.

articles.title: How to clear your Android phone cache - and why it greatly improves performance
articles.summary: Clearing your Android phone cache is a simple tweak that instantly boosts speed and responsiveness on almost any device.

articles.title: Don't plug these 7 common household gadgets into an extension cord - according to an electrician
articles.summary: Certain household appliances should not be plugged into extension cords due to safety risks, especially in cold weather. Larger devices require direct outlets to prevent electrical hazards.

articles.title: Adjusting my router's antenna fixed my Wi-Fi problems - here's the best way to position it
articles.summary: Strategic antenna positioning and router placement significantly improve Wi-Fi performance. Proper orientation requires experimentation to find the best signal strength.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving beyond dashboards. The article explores seven key trends expected to shape ML development in 2026.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: Agentic AI systems use state-managed interruptions to halt execution pipelines, implementing human-in-the-loop approval gates for autonomous agents.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference stages: prefill phase where attention processes prompts, decode phase generating predictions token-by-token, and KV cache optimization for efficient decoding.

articles.title: Building a Python Workflow That Catches Bugs Before Production
articles.summary: Modern tooling helps identify software defects earlier in the development lifecycle, preventing bugs from reaching production environments.

articles.title: Building Robust Credit Scoring Models with Python
articles.summary: A practical guide to building robust credit scoring models in Python, focusing on measuring variable relationships for effective feature selection in credit risk assessment.

articles.title: DenseNet Paper Walkthrough: All Connected
articles.summary: DenseNet addresses the vanishing gradient problem in very deep neural networks by improving weight updates during training. This architecture enables better model improvement through enhanced gradient flow.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights breakthroughs in physical AI and robotics during National Robotics Week, showcasing advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models that accelerate real-world deployment across industries.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient open models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a new 3D real-time strategy game expanding the acclaimed series.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals enables evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions beyond simple single-turn inputs. The framework assesses agent performance through helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage metrics.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation models using Amazon SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanding context windows, reducing training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables secure AI agent web access through domain filtering via AWS Network Firewall, preventing unauthorized data exfiltration and malware risks while maintaining agent functionality.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools accelerate vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages to match improved model throughput.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models launched to scale from data centers to edge devices. Designed to meet growing demand for AI deployment across the spectrum.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables sub-microsecond latency AI inference for algorithmic trading, allowing firms to compete in high-speed electronic markets with advanced neural network models instead of traditional FPGAs.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background innovations in extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse perspectives drive cutting-edge development in XR field.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and transparency. This work advances understanding of how LLMs make decisions, contributing to safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to imaging system design, using an encoder and noise model to quantify measurement quality and optimize optical systems for better image distinction.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
